author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
nitāi⋅pada⋅kamala, koṭicandra⋅suśītala,
je chāyāya jagat juḍāya
hena nitāi vine bhāi, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa pāite nāi,
dṛḍha kari’ dhara nitāira pāya

se⋅sambandha nāhi jā’ra, vṛthā janma gela tā’ra,
sei paśu baḍa durācāra
nitāi nā balila mukhe, majila saṁsāra⋅sukhe,
vidyākule ki karibe tā’ra

ahaṅkāre matta haiñā, nitāi⋅pada pāsariyā,
asatyare satya kari’ māni
nitāiyera karuṇā ha’be, vraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa pā’be,
dhara nitāiyera caraṇa du’khāni

nitāiyera caraṇa satya, tā̐hāra sevaka nitya,
nitāi⋅pada sadā kara āśa
narottama baḍa duḥkhī, nitāi more kara sukhī,
rākha rāṅgā caraṇera pāśa
en_translation:
Nitāi’s lotus feet are as cool and soothing as millions of moons; in their shade, the whole world finds peace. O brother, without such a Nitāi, one cannot attain Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa. Therefore, firmly hold on to Nitāi’s feet.

One who has no connection with Nitāi has wasted their life; that (two legged) animal is most wicked. If one does not utter Nitāi’s name and is absorbed in worldly pleasures, what good will their knowledge or family lineage do for them?

(That person) Being intoxicated with false ego, forgets Nitāi’s feet, and acceps untruth as truth. By Nitāi’s mercy, one will attain Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa in Vraja. Therefore, hold on to Nitāi’s two feet.

Nitāi’s feet are the ultimate truth, and His servants are eternal. Always yearn for Nitāi’s feet. Narottama is very unhappy; O Nitāi, please make me happy, and keep me by your reddish lotus feet.
